questions.question: Describe the difference between a linear block code and a non-linear block code. Provide an example of each. (15 marks)
questions.hint: Linear codes are used for data transmission over noisy channels, while non-linear codes are used for data storage and security.

questions.question: What is the purpose of cyclic redundancy check (CRC) in computer networking? (10 marks)
questions.hint: CRC is used to detect errors in data transmission by calculating a unique checksum value.

questions.question: Explain how a cyclic code works in terms of error detection and correction. Provide an example of a cyclic code. (20 marks)
questions.hint: Cyclic codes use a generator polynomial to encode data and detect errors. They can also correct single-bit errors.
